questions.question_text: What is the name of the examination?
What is the date of the examination?
What is the time of the examination?
What is the duration of the examination?
What is the total number of marks for the examination?
What is the name of the authority conducting the examination?
questions.student_answer: National Examination and School Inspection Authority

questions.question_text: Select and circle a tool that is used for transport on a farm.

questions.student_answer: used to measure length of the wood

questions.question_text: State the use of the following carpentry tools: b) A wood saw

State whether the following statements are True or False. b) A simple machine uses a single applied force to do work against single load.

questions.student_answer: Cutting tools: wood saw, Drilling tools: brace, Smoothing tools: plane

questions.student_answer: Store tools properly. Handle tools with much care.

questions.question_text: Choose the best alternative from the brackets to fill the gap.
Select the best definition of sound.
questions.student_answer: C) Energy which enables us to hear.

questions.question_text: Oxygen supports the burning of wood.
Trees provide oxygen.

Why is it not advisable to stay in a closed room with a burning charcoal stove?
questions.student_answer: because burning charcoals produces carbon monoxide which is poisonous

questions.question_text: The District of Gakenke is hilly. How do farmers in Gakenke overcome the problem of soil erosion?

questions.student_answer:
a) Clay soil -> i) Good for crops
b) Sand soil -> ii) Modeling pots
c) Loam soil -> iii) Making bricks

questions.question_text: State whether the following statements are True or False.
Match the following wastes with their sources.
questions.student_answer:
a) Used engine oil -> i) Agricultural waste
b) Raw sewage -> ii) Waste from automobiles
c) Plant remains -> iii) Industrial waste

i) Poor hygiene.
iv) Vaccinating chickens.

questions.question_text: a) Tongue enables us to balance our body.
b) Retina is the outer layer of the eye.

Match the deficiency diseases with their causes.
questions.student_answer:
a) Marasmus - i) Lack of iodine in diet.
b) Rickets - iii) Lack of vitamin D calcium.
c) Goitre - ii) Lack of enough food.

questions.question_text: Explain how you can properly maintain the respiratory system.
questions.student_answer:
Doing regular physical exercise
Drinking enough water
Avoid smoking
Get vaccinated

questions.question_text: Electrician wears gloves to prevent electric shock.
The switch is used to open and close the circuit.
Solar panels change sunlight into... form of energy.
...... is the boiling point of water while...... is the melting point of ice.
Match the parts of the simple electric circuit with their roles.
questions.student_answer:
a) Dry cell - i) break and complete the circuit
b) Bulb - iv) converts electrical energy into light energy
c) Wire - ii) transmit electric current
d) Switch - iii) produce energy.
